response:
Phase one needs ten man-hours while phase two requires twenty man-hours
Phase three takes forty man-hours and phase four needs eighty man-hours
Phase five demands one hundred sixty man-hours for the final work
Total time was twenty weeks before the delay occurred
Each phase now takes one point five times the original duration
New total duration equals thirty weeks for the whole project
This change impacts the schedule but not the phase order
Teams must adjust their plans to meet the new timeline
Resource allocation remains fixed despite the time increase
Fortune favors those who adapt quickly to such shifts in plans
